target.title: fix: recover Codex binding after stale preflight compaction
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86211.

This PR makes the Telegram/Codex preflight compaction path recover from stale native harness thread bindings instead of aborting inbound dispatch before an assistant run can start.

Changes:
- treats structured stale_thread_binding / missing_thread_binding native harness compaction failures as recoverable during preflight compaction
- keeps non-binding compaction failures fail-closed and visible
- lets Codex binding recovery create a fresh app-server thread when the old binding has already been cleared
- adds focused regression coverage for both the preflight dispatch path and the cleared-binding Codex recovery path

<!-- plaintext-proof-summary-start -->
## Plaintext Proof Summary

Updated: `2026-05-25T03:44:30Z`

This PR now includes the code repair ClawSweeper requested for the current head `a9ad8a918eba2b33d2766a8fda90a59fa0c30110`.

Code repair made after ClawSweeper review:
- Native Codex compaction no longer deletes a stale app-server binding before inbound recovery can reuse it.
- The stale binding remains available long enough for recovery to preserve `authProfileId`, `model`, `approvalPolicy`, `sandbox`, and `serviceTier` when creating the replacement thread.
- The replacement thread still overwrites the stale binding after successful recovery, so stale state is not kept after recovery succeeds.
- The compact regression now proves stale binding metadata is preserved when native compaction reports `thread not found`.

Validation passed after rebasing on current `origin/main`:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts`
- `npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts`
- `npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`

OpenClaw proof evidence on this repaired head:
- Head: `a9ad8a918eba2b33d2766a8fda90a59fa0c30110`
- Passing `Real behavior proof` workflow: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26381456712
- Second passing refresh: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26381466564
- Workflow log result: `External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.`

Known limitation:
- Maintainer-run live Telegram/Mantis proof is still blocked for this contributor account by repository authorization. The current branch now addresses the auth/model/sandbox metadata correctness blocker and includes plaintext validation evidence in this PR summary.
<!-- plaintext-proof-summary-end -->

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Inbound Telegram/Codex dispatch no longer aborts when preflight compaction sees a stale or missing Codex app-server thread binding. The recoverable stale-thread or missing-thread result now returns the current session entry so dispatch can continue, and the binding layer creates a fresh app-server thread if the binding file has already been cleared.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at /root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-upstream on Linux with Node 22.22.2, branch fix-86211-missing-thread-preflight, commit a9ad8a918eba2b33d2766a8fda90a59fa0c30110, rebased on current origin/main.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran targeted OpenClaw runtime checks in the local checkout: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts; focused npx oxlint on the changed core and extension files.

Evidence after fix: Local terminal output showed agent-runner-memory.test.ts passed with 27 tests, conversation-binding.test.ts passed with 13 tests, compact.test.ts passed with 26 tests, cli-compaction.test.ts passed with 11 tests, and focused oxlint exited 0 for the changed core and extension files. The command output was captured after rebasing on origin/main; PR text uses only redacted placeholders such as <codex-thread-id> and no chat IDs.

Observed result after fix: The stale-binding and missing-binding preflight regressions no longer throw "Preflight compaction required but failed: thread not found: <codex-thread-id>" or the equivalent missing-binding failure for structured stale_thread_binding or missing_thread_binding results. Non-binding failures still throw. The cleared-binding Codex conversation regression creates a fresh thread/start, runs turn/start on that new thread, returns a reply, and saves the new binding.

What was not tested: Live Telegram group delivery against a production gateway running this PR build was not tested. The proof uses the local OpenClaw checkout and targeted runtime paths with sanitized evidence. I requested the repository Mantis Telegram proof path in PR comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86216#issuecomment-4530350483, but direct workflow dispatch requires maintainer/admin rights.

## Validation

Passed locally:
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts
- npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
- npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts

Also ran node scripts/check-changed.mjs. Its typecheck/core lint/guard steps passed; the broad extension lint subprocess was killed locally after several minutes, so I followed with focused extension oxlint on the changed files, which passed.

## OpenClaw proof gate and live proof status

The repository OpenClaw `Real behavior proof` workflow has re-evaluated the current PR head and passed its parser gate.

- Head: `a9ad8a918eba2b33d2766a8fda90a59fa0c30110`
- Run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26381456712
- Second refresh: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26381466564
- Job log result: `External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.`

ClawSweeper still requires live or redacted runtime evidence showing recovered Telegram/Codex inbound dispatch and visible Telegram reply after this fix. I posted the requested Mantis command in the format ClawSweeper asked for:

- Mantis request: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86216#issuecomment-4530736605
- Triggered Mantis Desktop Proof run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26377627915
- Triggered Mantis Telegram Live run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26377627889

Both Mantis runs stopped at the repository authorization gate because the commenter account has `read` permission, while Mantis requires `write`, `maintain`, or `admin` for issue-comment requests. A maintainer can unblock by re-posting the same `@openclaw-mantis telegram live proof ...` request from an authorized account, or by re-applying the existing `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` label from maintainer context.

Until that maintainer-side Mantis run publishes artifacts, the remaining merge blocker is live transport proof, not another code/test change.

<!-- latest-openclaw-proof-start -->
### Latest OpenClaw proof refresh

Updated: `2026-05-25T03:44:30Z`

- OpenClaw `Real behavior proof`: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26381456712
- Second refresh: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26381466564
- Head: `a9ad8a918eba2b33d2766a8fda90a59fa0c30110`
- Job log result: `External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.`
<!-- latest-openclaw-proof-end -->

## Privacy

All issue and PR evidence is sanitized. Production chat IDs, message IDs, and Codex thread IDs are redacted or replaced with placeholders.

target.title: fix(agents): strip plaintext model provider keys
target.body:
## Summary
- strip plaintext provider `apiKey` values after merge-mode preservation, source-managed enforcement, and provider compatibility transforms so old `models.json` keys cannot be reintroduced into the final prompt-facing catalog
- clean legacy catalog-only `models.json` plaintext provider keys before the empty-provider `skip` path can preserve them
- migrate existing `models.json`-only plaintext provider keys into the target agent's local `auth-profiles.json` before writing the stripped catalog when there is no configured provider key and no target-local auth profile for that provider
- keep inherited main-agent profiles and runtime/external overlays from suppressing local legacy `models.json` key migration for non-main agents
- migrate known/env-present legacy env-name catalog credentials such as `LITELLM_KEY` to env-backed auth-profile `keyRef`s, while preserving ambiguous env-shaped plaintext strings as inline local auth-profile keys
- preserve non-secret markers such as env SecretRef markers and custom-provider `secretref-managed` compatibility markers
- preserve current-main plugin metadata discovery and manifest-aware model ID normalization while routing providers through the prompt-safe stripping path
- add regression coverage for generated plaintext stripping, merge-mode stripping, catalog-only cleanup, existing-key migration, fail-closed behavior, inherited-profile migration boundaries, env-present keyRef migration, ambiguous env-shaped key preservation, pi model discovery compatibility, metadata discovery threading, and current-head write-path stabilization

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated and legacy `models.json` files should not persist plaintext provider `apiKey` values. Cleanup should preserve upgrade auth outside the prompt-facing catalog, including non-main agent local migration boundaries and env-shaped legacy values that must only become `keyRef`s when an env marker or observed env value proves they are env-backed. The rebased branch should also preserve current-main plugin metadata discovery and manifest-aware model normalization.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source tree matching PR head `d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702` (`treeMatchesRemoteHead: true`; local commit `7a143b411b76acb8046c5fa1c65823e27d0768e1` has the same tree because the remote commit was created through the GitHub Git Database API), macOS, Node.js `v24.1.0`, `/usr/bin/python3` for fs-safe pinned writes. Provider keys in proof output are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=require FS_SAFE_PYTHON=/usr/bin/python3 REMOTE_HEAD=d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702 REMOTE_TREE=4497822feac55fee0e0442f93328e5da0e423cc5 node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

The inline Node proof script created temporary local agent dirs, called `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`, `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps`, `discoverModels`, `ensureAuthProfileStore`, and `createProviderAuthResolver`, and exercised these real paths: inline custom provider stripping, legacy configured `models.json` key migration, catalog-only cleanup, env-present `keyRef` migration, ambiguous env-shaped plaintext preservation, non-main inherited-profile migration boundary, pi model discovery compatibility, and manifest-aware model normalization after stripping.

- Evidence after fix (copied live output, redacted):

```json
{
  "remoteHead": "d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702",
  "localCommit": "7a143b411b76acb8046c5fa1c65823e27d0768e1",
  "treeMatchesRemoteHead": true,
  "localTree": "4497822feac55fee0e0442f93328e5da0e423cc5",
  "tempRoot": "<tmp>",
  "inlineCustomProvider": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "discoverModelsFindsCustomModel": "custom-model"
  },
  "legacyModelsJsonOnlyUpgrade": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "discoverModelsFindsLegacyModel": "legacy-model"
  },
  "catalogOnlyLegacyCleanup": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "discoverModelsFindsCatalogOnlyModel": "catalog-only-model"
  },
  "envPresentCatalogMigration": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "hasInlineKey": false,
      "keyRef": {
        "source": "env",
        "provider": "default",
        "id": "LITELLM_KEY"
      },
      "copyToAgents": false
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "apiKeyMarker": "LITELLM_KEY",
      "resolvedEnvValueSeen": true
    }
  },
  "ambiguousEnvShapedCredential": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "hasInlineKey": true,
      "hasKeyRef": false,
      "copyToAgents": false
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "preservedLocalKeySeen": true
    }
  },
  "inheritedProfileBoundary": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "localMigratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "resolvedWorkerKey": true
    }
  },
  "manifestAwareModelNormalization": {
    "planAction": "write",
    "normalizedModelId": "plugin-prefix/bare-model",
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the final `models.json` omits plaintext keys; non-built-in custom providers with inline model rows retain the non-secret `secretref-managed` marker; old configured and catalog-only `models.json` credentials migrate into `auth-profiles.json`; env-present legacy catalog values migrate as env-backed `keyRef` credentials and resolve through provider auth; ambiguous env-shaped legacy values with no env signal are preserved as inline local auth-profile keys instead of unresolved env refs; a non-main agent with an inherited same-profile ID still migrates its local legacy catalog key and runtime auth resolves the worker-local key; pi `discoverModels` can still find custom model rows; and manifest-aware model normalization still threads plugin policies through the stripped catalog path.
- What was not tested: I did not test a real paid third-party provider. The live proof did not force auth-profile persistence failure; that fail-closed path is covered by the focused regression test listed below.

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- merge-ref simulation against local `origin/main` with the same three targeted tests after merging the current proof/fix tree
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.core.json`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/models-config.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-registry.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.types.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.types.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/models-config.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-registry.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.types.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.types.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Refs #11829

target.title: fix(gateway): add provider auth prewarm operator controls
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: provider auth prewarm can still be expensive during gateway startup or recovery, and affected operators do not have a supported way to disable or delay it.
- Solution: expose a gateway-level provider auth prewarm policy with config and env controls.
- What changed: added `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled`, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.delayMs`, and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM`; startup, reload, logout-triggered rewarm, delayed startup prewarm, and auth-profile-failure rewarm paths now honor the resolved policy.
- Docs: documented the new config keys and env override in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and updated the generated config baseline hash.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not claim to fix the underlying event-loop starvation in `warmCurrentProviderAuthState`, Discord READY timing, or OpenAI/Codex route fallback behavior.

## Motivation

This PR follows up on the Discord/operator recovery report in #85997. It also relates to the narrower provider-auth startup/prewarm reproduction in #85999.

This proposes an operator safety valve while maintainers evaluate the deeper scheduling/caching fix for provider auth prewarm. It is intentionally not marked as closing either issue because the user-facing reports cover broader startup, channel readiness, and auth-provider routing behavior.

AI-assisted: drafted and implemented with Codex; human review and maintainer product feedback are requested before merge.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Follow-up to #85997.
- Related to #85999.
- This PR does not close either issue; it proposes a scoped mitigation for provider auth prewarm operator control.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: affected deployments can now disable provider auth prewarm or increase its startup delay without patching OpenClaw internals.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu Linux server. Startup proof was captured on this branch before the final sidecar-policy repair; the branch is now rebased on `origin/main` at `483d7be6c4` with current head `3f3fb0361c`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Started a real gateway process from this checkout with an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
  - Used `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1` to keep the run hermetic while still exercising the gateway startup/post-ready path.
  - Ran three redacted startup cases: default config, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: false`, and config-enabled plus `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
- Evidence after fix (redacted terminal/log proof):

Default config still schedules and runs provider auth prewarm:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=1.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] provider auth state pre-warmed in 75ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] default-enabled elapsedMs=16851 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

Config disable skips the provider auth prewarm sidecar:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=0.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.maintenance 13.5ms total=5670.5ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] config-disabled elapsedMs=10409 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

Env override skips prewarm even when config enables it:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=0.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.maintenance 13.3ms total=5482.6ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] env-disabled elapsedMs=10152 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

- Targeted verification:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts` -> 3 files passed, 88 tests passed after final rebase.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 111 tests passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm config:docs:check` -> passed after final rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` -> passed after final rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
- Observed result after fix: default behavior remains enabled; config/env disable paths skip startup prewarm; delayed startup prewarm and auth-profile-failure rewarm re-check the current policy before running; reload and logout rewarm paths skip when the same policy disables provider auth prewarm.
- What was not tested: live Discord restart timing, real provider CLI starvation on a constrained VM, and OpenAI Codex OAuth fallback behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #85999 includes startup prewarm timing logs; #85997 includes redacted Discord/operator-visible errors.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: current main already defers provider auth prewarm, but the exposed prewarm controls are internal test/runtime options rather than an operator-facing config surface.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no schema-covered config/env option to disable or delay provider auth prewarm on deployments where the prewarm path harms startup or channel recovery.
- Contributing context (if known): #85272 narrowed startup ordering by deferring prewarm; #85999 reports that provider auth prewarm can still monopolize startup on a constrained host.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`, `src/config/config-misc.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: disabled prewarm policy skips startup/reload/logout rewarm paths, env override disables config-enabled prewarm, configured delay is honored, and a long-lived provider-auth sidecar re-checks current config before delayed startup or auth-profile-failure rewarm.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the change is policy resolution and call-site gating; these tests cover each touched call path without requiring live providers.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): startup prewarm ordering coverage existed, but not operator-facing config/env policy.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds optional gateway config and env controls:

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "providerAuthPrewarm": {
      "enabled": false,
      "delayMs": 5000
    }
  }
}
```

`OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1` disables provider auth prewarm regardless of config.

Defaults remain unchanged: provider auth prewarm stays enabled with the existing startup delay.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [provider auth prewarm always scheduled]

After:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [resolve config/env policy] -> [schedule or skip provider auth prewarm]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu Linux server
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: provider behavior mocked in tests; real gateway startup proof used isolated temp config/state
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway startup/reload/logout paths; no live Discord run
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic gateway config with `auth.mode: none`, `controlUi.enabled: false`, temporary port, and no real channel/provider credentials

### Steps

1. Start gateway with default synthetic config and startup trace enabled.
2. Start gateway with `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: false`.
3. Start gateway with `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: true` plus `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
4. Run targeted gateway/config tests, config docs check, and core typecheck.

### Expected

- Default policy schedules and runs provider auth prewarm.
- Disabled policy skips provider auth prewarm.
- Env override wins over config-enabled prewarm.
- Configured delay controls startup prewarm delay.
- Long-lived sidecar rewarm paths respect the current config after hot reload.

### Actual

- Matches expected in redacted real gateway startup logs, targeted tests, local typechecks, and docs check.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real gateway startup proof for default/config-disabled/env-disabled behavior; targeted gateway/config tests; docs baseline check; `tsgo:core`; `tsgo:core:test`; `tsgo:extensions:test`.
- Edge cases checked: env override disabling config-enabled prewarm; invalid schema values rejected; reload/logout rewarm paths honor the same policy; long-lived sidecar skips delayed startup and auth-profile-failure rewarm after current config disables prewarm.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord startup recovery, real constrained-host prewarm timing, and provider OAuth fallback behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration required; operators may opt into the new config/env controls.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: disabling prewarm may make model/provider auth status colder until first use.
  - Mitigation: default behavior remains enabled; the disable path is explicit opt-in for affected operators.
- Risk: this may be considered a product/config surface decision rather than the preferred root fix.
  - Mitigation: scoped as a mitigation, and does not close the linked issues.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 1 protocol foundation
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the narrow foundation. It creates the versioned broker SDK subpath and bundled channel-broker plugin, but it does not move Telegram, Discord, Slack, or any user defaults. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 1 - SDK and bundled broker foundation - Role: Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. - Head: `b95fe03a87` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `b95fe03a87` Focused local validation after the latest Phase 1 package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary Adds Phase 1 of the Channel Broker consolidation program: a versioned `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol surface, a bundled `channel-broker` channel plugin, provider-owned outbound request/receipt helpers, docs, and focused tests. This PR intentionally stays at the protocol/foundation layer. Telegram, Discord, Slack, and later channel migrations are tracked separately and should not be folded into this PR. ## Linked Context Closes #86107 Related #86105 Related #86108 Related #86109 Related #86110 Related #86111 Related #86112 Related #86113 Related #86114 Related #86115 ## Review Repair History The current Phase 1 branch includes focused repairs for ClawSweeper's blocker classes: - Preserves native prefix ownership when `channel-broker` declares platform aliases that overlap native plugins. If Telegram/Discord/Slack are registered, their prefixes stay native-owned; broker aliases own a platform prefix only when no native plugin declares it. - Adds the standard channel SecretRef contract for `channels.channel-broker.{outboundToken,signingSecret}` plus `accounts.*` and `providers.*` variants, and changes the broker schema/docs to use SecretRef-compatible credential input. - Threads `AbortSignal` through broker outbound requests, including the HTTP `fetch` transport and custom runtime override path. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Verified the Phase 1 broker surface resolves as a bundled channel and produces a normalized V1 outbound request for a Telegram-scoped broker target. This proves the user-visible foundation being shipped in this PR: catalog discovery, channel id normalization, target construction, protocol versioning, platform normalization, thread preservation, and final-send payload shape. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, after the current Phase 1 repair/doc series through `d39d47b3db`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); const target = buildBrokerConversationTarget({ platform: "Telegram", conversationId: "chat 123", threadId: "topic-7" }); const request = createBrokerOutboundRequest({ requestId: "send-proof-1", providerId: "synthetic-provider", platform: "Telegram", accountId: "default", conversation: { id: "chat 123", type: "channel", threadId: "topic-7" }, mode: "final", payloads: [{ text: "broker proof" }], requirements: { text: true, thread: true } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));' ``` - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above: ```json { "discoveredChannel": { "id": "channel-broker", "order": 60, "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker" }, "channelNormalization": "channel-broker", "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7", "outboundProtocolVersion": 1, "outboundMode": "final", "outboundPlatform": "telegram", "outboundConversation": { "id": "chat 123", "type": "channel", "threadId": "topic-7" }, "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof" } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** OpenClaw discovered the bundled `channel-broker` entry, normalized the channel id, built a Telegram broker target that preserved `threadId`, and emitted a V1 final outbound request with normalized `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.threadId: "topic-7"`, and payload text `broker proof`. - **What was not tested:** No real third-party broker provider delivery was tested in Phase 1. That is intentionally deferred to the Phase 2 migration proof issues for Telegram, Discord, and Slack. ## Focused Local Validation All local validation below ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: ```bash git diff --check ``` ```bash node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/runtime.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ## Known Inherited Gates - `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check` has an inherited clean-upstream SDK budget drift; this PR adds the intended `plugin-sdk/channel-broker` public subpath. - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` has inherited unrelated glossary-label failures on clean upstream; Channel Broker glossary entries were added in this branch.

commands.author: Kaspre

target.title: fix(cli): enforce local agent hard timeout
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw agent --local --timeout N` can still leave the CLI alive until an outer wrapper timeout kills it. The lower-level embedded agent timeout is useful, but it is not a command-level wall-clock guarantee: setup, plugin registration, retries, cleanup, or a wedged embedded run can keep the CLI process around after the operator-specified timeout window.

## Change and value

This PR adds a CLI-level hard timeout for embedded local agent runs. Explicit `--local` runs and embedded fallback runs now share a wall-clock boundary of the resolved finite timeout plus 30 seconds of grace. If that timer fires, the CLI aborts the local run signal and exits with code `124`, matching the exit code operators already see from GNU `timeout` wrappers.

The value is that scripts no longer need to wait for their own much larger timeout window just because the embedded run failed to settle cleanly.

## Who is affected, and who is not

- Affected: operators and automation running `openclaw agent --local` with a finite `--timeout` or configured agent timeout.
- Affected: gateway-backed runs only after they enter embedded fallback; the normal Gateway request path keeps its existing timeout and `chat.abort` behavior.
- Not affected: `--timeout 0` or a configured local agent timeout of `0`; those continue to mean no CLI hard timeout.
- Not fixed here: post-success event-loop handles that keep the process alive after a successful local result. That is covered separately by #86264.

## Why now

Local diagnostics showed the no-completion CLI-exit class directly: an `agent --local` run can fail to produce a final result before the outer wrapper kills it, even when the inner timeout is shorter. This patch makes the local CLI boundary own that wall-clock guarantee instead of relying only on the embedded runner to unwind itself.

## Implementation

- Arms a startup local-agent hard timer before full plugin/command registration, so `agent --local --timeout N` is bounded even if setup hangs before the agent command action is reached.
- When `--timeout` is omitted, resolves the startup boundary from `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` before command/plugin registration instead of using an unconditional fallback; explicit `--timeout` still wins, and `--timeout 0` disables the timer.
- Keeps the lower-level embedded-run helper boundary around the existing `agentCommand` call sites.
- Uses the resolved finite timeout plus 30s as the hard deadline, with `0` disabling the timer.
- Forwards the resolved timeout into `agentCommand`, so cooperative embedded abort and the outer hard boundary agree on the same timeout.
- Preserves `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM` precedence: signals still exit promptly with `130`/`143`, even if the embedded run ignores abort or rejects during teardown.
- On timeout, aborts the signal, writes a concise timeout error, and exits through `runtime.exit(124)`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw agent --local --timeout N` and embedded fallback runs should have a command-level wall-clock timeout even if setup or the local run fails to settle after lower-level aborts.

Real environment tested: Linux/WSL local OpenClaw setup. Before baseline was global OpenClaw `2026.5.20 (e510042)` with the local `cli-exit-fix` patch still applied. Current PR head is `6befa4a1a0c1157930fa632f921d9092a4ea0fb4`. The original live timeout proof was captured on #86276 head `e0e10b389dc3c08b27890db5d78e47d3fc9ef58f`; the follow-up configured-timeout correction on `6befa4a1a0c1157930fa632f921d9092a4ea0fb4` is covered by the focused regression tests listed in Verification. Combined stack proof used #86276 `e0e10b389dc3c08b27890db5d78e47d3fc9ef58f` plus #86264 `98802c0f63c0a4fcc8639b8dde535f3bed879207`, combined head `6a806f8f19628c16f99627162b8f53a01e27e680`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
timeout 70s node /tmp/openclaw-combined-package/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent eval-1 --timeout 1 --model ollama-cloud/glm-5.1 -m 'Reply with exactly the word: PONG' --json
timeout 300s node /tmp/openclaw-cli-exit-combined/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent eval-1 --timeout 180 --model ollama-cloud/glm-5.1 -m 'Reply with exactly the word: PONG' --json
timeout 240s node /tmp/openclaw-cli-exit-combined/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id combined-cli-exit-proof --message 'Return marker OPENCLAW_CLI_EXIT_COMBINED_PROOF_OK' --thinking off --json
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal transcripts and copied console output were captured under `/home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/2026-05-25-cli-exit-combined/`, including `25-after-combined-package-early-boundary-timeout1.out`, `31-after-combined-source-success-pong.out`, and `32-after-combined-source-mock-success.out`.

```text
local agent command timed out after 1s plus 30s grace
EXIT_CODE=124
ELAPSED_SEC=39

[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] ... phase=attempt-dispatch ...
local agent command timed out after 180s plus 30s grace
EXIT_CODE=124
ELAPSED_SEC=212

finalAssistantVisibleText: OPENCLAW_CLI_EXIT_COMBINED_PROOF_OK
executionTrace.runner: embedded
executionTrace.attempts[0].result: success
mock request path: /v1/responses
EXIT_CODE=0
ELAPSED_SEC=41
ASSERT_AGENT_TURN=passed
```

Observed result after fix: The `--timeout 1` local-agent command exited from OpenClaw's own hard boundary with code `124` after the configured timeout plus grace, instead of falling through to the outer wrapper. A longer live local-agent run also exited from OpenClaw's own `180s + 30s` boundary. The combined success-path run produced a final embedded-agent result and exited normally with code `0`.

Before evidence: On the production baseline, the local workaround was still firing instead of upstream owning the timeout.

```bash
timeout 70s openclaw agent --local --agent eval-1 --timeout 1 --model ollama-cloud/glm-5.1 -m 'Reply with exactly the word: PONG' --json
```

```text
[openclaw] cli-exit-fix: hard wall-clock SIGKILL after 31s (timeout=1s + 30s grace)
EXIT_CODE=1
ELAPSED_SEC=40
```

Before evidence optional: During review, old draft #86276 head `8790ec43051f30fb48a5b8772ff4f3671f9307cd2` stacked with #86264 still exited only from an outer `timeout 70s` wrapper and did not print OpenClaw's timeout message. A plugin trace showed repeated pre-agent discovery/registration work, proving the first draft armed the boundary too late.

What was not tested: a standalone package install from this exact branch without #86264. The success-path exit proof above is intentionally run on the combined stack because #86264 owns the post-completion cleanup class.

## Verification

Standalone #86276 head `6befa4a1a0c1157930fa632f921d9092a4ea0fb4`:

- `env OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 600s node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/cli/run-main.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (41/41; covers configured omitted-timeout resolver, explicit override precedence, and configured `0` disabling)
- `env OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 600s node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (90/90; covers omitted `--timeout` plus configured timeout before command registration)
- `env OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 600s node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (54/54)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

Combined stack `6a806f8f19628c16f99627162b8f53a01e27e680`:

- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test:unit:fast -- src/cli/run-main.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

## Relationship to #86264

#86264 handles the post-completion cleanup class: a command has finished but deferred context-engine maintenance keeps work alive. This PR handles the no-completion class: a local/embedded agent command never settles, so the CLI needs a hard wall-clock boundary. The combined mock-agent proof verifies the two fixes compose: successful local results still exit normally, while wedged/no-completion runs are capped by this PR.

AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Control UI could restore a historical routed session key like `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` from persisted local settings after reload.
- Why it matters: users would reopen Control UI and land in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI look misrouted even though the backend heartbeat target remained main.
- What changed: session restore now sanitizes persisted routed direct/group session keys during settings load and falls back to `main` instead.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway routing behavior, heartbeat behavior, or live session switching logic was changed.

- [x] Bug fix

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #61661
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI restoring historical routed direct/group sessions from persisted local settings instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm vitest run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
✓ ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts ... falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       18 passed (18)
```
- Observed result after fix: persisted `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` session selections are normalized back to `main` during settings restore.
- What was not tested: explicit session selection through URL query params or live backend routing changes, because this PR only changes local restore behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Control UI storage restore trusted persisted `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` values without filtering out historical routed direct/group sessions.
- Missing detection / guardrail: storage tests covered scoped session persistence but did not cover stale routed-session restore behavior.
- Contributing context (if known): main-session normalization already existed later in the gateway boot path, but the bad routed session had already won at the storage restore layer.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

  - [x] Unit test

- Scenario the test should lock in: persisted routed `direct`/`group` session keys fall back to `main` on Control UI restore.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug originates in local persisted settings load, so the storage tests are the exact boundary where the regression occurs.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds two restore-specific cases for routed `direct` and `group` sessions.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

After reload/reopen, Control UI will no longer restore stale routed `direct`/`group` sessions from persisted local settings; it will fall back to the main session instead.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI local storage restore

### Steps

1. Seed persisted Control UI settings with a routed direct/group session key in `sessionsByGateway`.
2. Call `loadSettings()`.
3. Verify restored `sessionKey` / `lastActiveSessionKey` fall back to `main`.

### Expected

- routed historical sessions should not win restore by default
- restored settings should point back to main

### Actual

- both new routed-session storage tests pass
- `tsgo:core` passes
- `lint:core` passes

Attach at least one:

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the restore sanitization happens in `ui/src/ui/storage.ts`, which is the correct layer for this bug.
- Verified routed `direct` and `group` session keys now restore to `main` in storage tests.
- Verified core type/lint checks pass on the changed files.

## Review Conversations

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a user might intentionally want to reopen a routed direct/group session from persisted state.
  - Mitigation: this PR only changes the automatic restore default; explicit session selection can still be handled through current UI/navigation flows.
- Risk: over-matching could reset legitimate session keys.
  - Mitigation: the sanitization is narrowly limited to `agent:*:*:(direct|group):*` patterns only.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  ui  ../../ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts (22 tests) 38ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  22 passed (22)
    Start at  00:14:43
    Duration  611ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Routed direct/group session keys persisted in localStorage were not cleared on restore. The regex `/^agent:[^:]+:[^:]+:(direct|group):.+$/` only matched 5-segment per-channel-peer keys, missing `agent:<id>:direct:<peer>` (per-peer) and `agent:<id>:<channel>:<acct>:direct:<peer>` (per-account-channel-peer) shapes emitted by `buildAgentPeerSessionKey`.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, jsdom, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 22/22 tests pass including two new tests covering per-peer (`agent:main:direct:ou_123`) and per-account-channel-peer (`agent:main:feishu:acct_abc:direct:ou_123`) shapes, both correctly falling back to `main`.
**Observed result after fix:** All three `buildAgentPeerSessionKey` direct shapes and group shapes reset to main on storage restore.
**What was not tested:** Live Control UI reload in a browser with actual localStorage entries; all three direct key shapes verified via jsdom storage unit tests.

## Current-head follow-up proof — 2026-05-20

- Covers ClawSweeper's threaded per-peer routed session gap for agent:<id>:direct:<peer>:thread:<id> and legacy dm equivalents.

~~~text
{"currentHead":"156d49e17c","seededSession":"agent:main:dm:12345:thread:12345:99","loadedGatewayUrl":"wss://gateway.example:8443","restoredSessionKey":"main","restoredLastActiveSessionKey":"main","restoredToMain":true}
~~~

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 4 constrained provider capabilities
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR keeps WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles honest by making hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed constraints visible instead of pretending they are equivalent official bot APIs. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 4 - Constrained/device-bound provider capabilities - Role: Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. - Head: `f2c8254534` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `f2c8254534` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds Phase 4 constrained/device-bound provider metadata to the Channel Broker contract. - Covers WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles-backed iMessage bridges as broker provider capabilities, not native plugin rewrites. - Stacks on Phase 3 official/app capability matrix (#86164), Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86114 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86148, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds `constraints` and `badges` to broker platform/provider capabilities. - Models constrained provider facts such as: - `businessApi`, `cloudApi`, `providerHosted` - `deviceBound`, `linkedDevice`, `qrPairing`, `sessionFragile` - `selfHosted`, `phoneNumberRequired`, `signalCli` - `macHostRequired`, `messagesSignedIn`, `privateApiOptional`, `privateApiRequired` - `externalBridge` - Exposes resolved provider capabilities in channel-broker status snapshots so UI/status can show constrained-provider badges. - Adds WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage target proof through `channel-broker`. - Documents constrained provider expectations in Channel Broker, SDK, WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles migration docs. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Constrained providers expose hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed limits through broker `constraints`/`badges`, and WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage still route through provider-owned broker requests. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that evaluated constrained-provider capability support and called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` for `broker:whatsapp:direct:15551234567`, `broker:signal:direct:+15551234567`, and `broker:imessage:group:chat-guid-1?threadId=imessage-thread-1`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "constrainedProviders": { "whatsappBusiness": true, "whatsappDeviceBound": false, "signalDeviceBound": true }, "outbound": [ { "label": "whatsapp-direct", "platform": "whatsapp", "conversation": { "id": "15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "signal-direct", "platform": "signal", "conversation": { "id": "+15551234567", "type": "direct" } }, { "label": "imessage-group", "platform": "imessage", "conversation": { "id": "chat-guid-1", "type": "group", "threadId": "imessage-thread-1" } } ] } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path distinguished WhatsApp Business-style support from device-bound WhatsApp requirements, accepted Signal's self-hosted/device-bound constraint set, and produced broker provider requests for WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage. - **What was not tested:** Live WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, or BlueBubbles-backed provider traffic; this PR proves the broker contract/schema/docs layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/status.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md docs/channels/whatsapp.md docs/channels/signal.md docs/channels/imessage.md docs/channels/imessage-from-bluebubbles.md` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not implement Phase 5 legacy-plugin freeze. - Next phase: #86115 freezes new legacy native-channel feature work after broker migration paths exist. This PR supplies the constrained-provider metadata needed before that freeze can be credible.

Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2A - Conformance and signed inbound baseline - Role: Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff with explicit listed-provider gating. - Head: `f6bf429242` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `f6bf429242` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2A Channel Broker conformance baseline on top of #86096. - Extends `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` with inbound normalization, dedupe-key, provider capability normalization, and platform capability checks. - Adds bundled broker conformance coverage for durable inbound receive, durable final send receipts, retryable receipt failures, preview/progress capability gates, and `/verbose`/tool-message `channelData`. ## Rollout Context Closes #86109 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86133, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. This PR intentionally does not migrate Telegram, Discord, or Slack routing. Native defaults stay unchanged until the Phase 2B/2C/2D proofs land behind opt-in broker routing. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** The broker conformance baseline normalizes inbound events, builds durable dedupe keys, evaluates preview/progress capabilities, and sends outbound requests through the bundled broker lifecycle without platform-specific core branches. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that imported `src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts`, created a Telegram inbound event, built the durable dedupe key, evaluated Discord preview/progress capabilities, and sent broker outbound requests through `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "inboundDedupeKey": "acme:bot-main:telegram:live-proof-telegram-1", "previewProgressSupported": true } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path produced the expected durable inbound key and capability decision outside the Vitest harness, proving the Phase 2A contract surface can carry the normalized lifecycle facts that later channel migrations consume. - **What was not tested:** Live provider network traffic and the full local suite; those remain covered by later provider proofs and GitHub Actions. ### Behavior The broker contract now has a conformance harness for the lifecycle paths that regress most often across maintained chat channels: inbound normalization/dedupe, durable final sends, provider receipts, retry/failure propagation, preview/progress capability checks, and `/verbose` tool-message metadata. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-conformance` - Head: `f6bf429242` (current Phase 2A head) ### Steps 1. Normalized a synthetic Telegram inbound event through the SDK and accepted it through the durable inbound receive journal. 2. Replayed the same dedupe key while pending and after completion to prove duplicate/redelivery handling. 3. Sent a synthetic Slack final message through the bundled broker runtime and verified provider receipt commit material. 4. Returned a retryable Discord provider receipt and verified the send fails with structured receipt context. 5. Built preview/finalize broker requests and asserted Discord preview/progress support through declared capabilities. 6. Passed `/verbose` tool-message policy through broker `channelData` without platform-specific branching. ### Evidence - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - 1 file passed, 7 tests passed. - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 3 files passed, 10 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result The SDK helpers normalize provider payloads without dropping native metadata, the bundled plugin refuses non-sent provider receipts as durable-send failures, and conformance tests prove the current broker path can represent the Phase 2 lifecycle requirements without changing user-visible channel defaults. ### Not Tested - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. - Live Telegram, Discord, or Slack provider traffic. Those are intentionally scoped to Phase 2B/2C/2D. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.
