target.title: feat: add auto fast mode
target.body:
Fixes #85087

## Summary

- adds `fast: auto` alongside `fast: on/off`, with the default 60s cutoff and configurable `fast_seconds`
- resolves effective fast mode dynamically so follow-up model calls after the cutoff can run without priority/fast tier where the backend supports retiering
- emits channel-visible fast progress notices in the tool/progress stream: `💨Fast: auto-off(...)` at the next tool-execution boundary after the cutoff, then `💨Fast: auto-on` at turn reset
- shows the current effective fast mode directly in Telegram `/fast`, including the configured auto threshold in status text and the native menu button
- keeps Telegram progress logs visible when `streaming.progress.keepLog` is enabled
- updates TUI/UI/ACP/protocol/docs/tests for the new auto mode

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts src/agents/fast-mode.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.fast-mode-auto.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/slash-commands.browser-import.test.ts`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test:bundled`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false protocol:check`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false check:architecture`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false openclaw gateway restart`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false openclaw gateway status`
- Live Telegram Desktop proof via Computer Use against the managed LaunchAgent gateway from this checkout
- Earlier broad local `PATH="/tmp/openclaw-swiftlint:$PATH" pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false check:changed` passed through conflict markers, changelog attributions, dependency/package guards, core/core-test/extension/extension-test typecheck, core/extensions/scripts lint; local `lint apps` failed because portable SwiftLint cannot load SourceKit without full Xcode on this host

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `fast: auto` starts with effective fast mode enabled, disables fast mode for later model calls after the configured threshold, reports the cutoff in channel progress, resets with `💨Fast: auto-on`, and shows current fast status in Telegram `/fast` without requiring `/fast status`.

Real environment tested: local managed Gateway LaunchAgent, CLI/Gateway `2026.5.21`, Telegram plugin in a real Telegram Desktop DM, config `fastMode: auto` with `fast_seconds: 15`, real provider `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and shell tool calls around an ~18s sleep.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false openclaw gateway restart
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false openclaw gateway status
```

Telegram mixed-order prompt used after restart:

```text
Run exactly three shell tool calls, one at a time, wait for each output before next: first command `python3 -c 'print("tool_a_before")'`; second command `python3 -c 'import time; s=int(time.time()*1000); time.sleep(18); e=int(time.time()*1000); print(f"sleep_start_ms={s}"); print(f"sleep_end_ms={e}"); print(f"sleep_duration_ms={e-s}")'`; third command `python3 -c 'print("tool_b_after")'`. Do not combine commands. Do not use loops. Final reply exactly: tool_a_before, observed sleep_duration_ms, tool_b_after.
```

Evidence after fix: Telegram Desktop showed one progress draft containing normal shell tool lines before `💨Fast: auto-off(32s>=15s)`, then more normal shell tool lines before `💨Fast: auto-on`. The final Telegram reply reported `tool_a_before`, `sleep_duration_ms=18003`, and `tool_b_after`. Screenshots are intentionally not attached here because the live Telegram window contains PII; reviewer/maintainer can attach a redacted screenshot separately.

Observed result after fix: gateway status probe was ok on port `18789`, the managed LaunchAgent command pointed at this checkout's `dist/index.js`, the progress draft kept the fast events in the tool list, and the final reply arrived after all tool results.

What was not tested: local SwiftLint app lint, because portable SwiftLint on this host fails to load `sourcekitdInProc.framework` without full Xcode. CI macOS should cover that lane. Config hot reload for normal Telegram reply dispatch remains future work; per reviewer/user direction, this PR does not add per-message config rereads. In a real provider turn, `💨Fast: auto-on` is the end-of-turn reset event, so it is not expected to have a later tool line in the same turn; the Telegram renderer unit test covers the synthetic mixed-draft order where both fast callbacks are surrounded by ordinary tool lines.

target.title: feat(signal): support reaction approvals
target.body:
Summary

- Add Signal native exec/plugin approval capability gated by top-level approvals configuration.
- Add thumbs-up / thumbs-down reaction approval handling for allow-once and deny, with explicit Signal approver auth.
- Bind reactions only from native approval delivery, verify target-author identity, persist binding state, and re-check current approval route config before resolving.
- Harden reaction bindings by normalizing Signal UUID author keys and removing timestamp-only cross-conversation fallback.

Verification

- [x] node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts
- [x] pnpm check:changed
- [x] git diff --check
- [x] Manual dev.review loop passes addressed target route gating, target-author verification, generic send trust boundary, reaction hint binding, persisted route re-checks, and exact reaction binding scope.

Behavior addressed: Signal can deliver approval prompts through native approval delivery and resolve authorized 👍 / 👎 reactions as allow-once / deny without cross-conversation timestamp fallback.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts; pnpm check:changed; git diff --check
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 3 files / 24 tests; pnpm check:changed passed extension and extension-test lanes.
Observed result after fix: Signal UUID author-key casing matches, reaction binding stays exact to the conversation/message key, and changed-lane typecheck/lint checks pass.
What was not tested: live Signal transport/reaction roundtrip against a real Signal account in this fix pass.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 4 constrained provider capabilities
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR keeps WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles honest by making hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed constraints visible instead of pretending they are equivalent official bot APIs. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 3 official channel capability matrix
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR prevents the long tail from becoming a new pile of native branches. It expresses official app/API platforms as broker aliases and capability rows first, and it now enforces declared delivery capabilities before outbound handoff. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 3 - Official/app platform capability matrix - Role: Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. - Head: `f1a62e9f8a` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `f1a62e9f8a` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 3 official/app channel capability matrix for Channel Broker. - Keeps the work at the broker contract/catalog layer: no native channel migration defaults change in this PR. - Stacks on Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86113 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86144, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds canonical broker platform aliases while keeping `platform: string` open for provider-owned long-tail targets: - `teams` / `msteams` -> `microsoft-teams` - `googlechat` -> `google-chat` - `qq` -> `qqbot` - Aligns `channels.channel-broker.*.capabilities` config with the SDK capability shape: - `delivery` - `live` - `receive` - `native` - Adds focused proof for Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, LINE, Feishu, QQ bot, Zalo, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Twitch, IRC, Nostr, Tlon, and Synology Chat. - Documents the Phase 3 platform matrix and SDK capability shape. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Official/app channel aliases normalize through the broker catalog, and Phase 3 platforms route through provider-owned broker requests without enabling native migration defaults. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:teams:19:meeting-channel"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "teams-alias", "messageId": "native-teams-alias", "platform": "microsoft-teams", "conversation": { "id": "19:meeting-channel", "type": "channel" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path canonicalized the Teams alias to `microsoft-teams` and produced a provider-owned broker request with no platform-specific native extension branch. - **What was not tested:** Live Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, regional, or self-hosted provider traffic; this PR proves the broker catalog/schema/conformance layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` Note: `pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 ...` was unavailable in the local workspace, so the documented `pnpm dlx` path was used. It linted the docs glob cleanly. ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not move native routing defaults for any Phase 3 channel.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2C Discord opt-in broker routing proof
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Discord DMs, channels, and threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Discord remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2C - Discord opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. - Head: `370402e44f` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `370402e44f` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2C Discord broker routing proof on top of #86154, #86153, and #86096. - Normalizes broker-routed Discord DMs and channel threads without importing native Discord extension internals. - Preserves non-channel conversation type in canonical broker routes so Discord DMs remain direct routes. ## Rollout Context Closes #86111 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86154 Stacked on #86153 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86139, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. Native Discord defaults stay unchanged. Existing prefix ownership still keeps `discord:` owned by the native Discord plugin when installed; this PR proves the opt-in broker route through `broker:discord:...` and the broker alias path when native Discord is absent. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Discord DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and provider-owned ids. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:discord:user:123456789012345678"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "discord-dm", "messageId": "native-discord-dm", "platform": "discord", "conversation": { "id": "123456789012345678", "type": "direct" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced a direct Discord broker provider request rather than an ambiguous channel-looking target. - **What was not tested:** Live Discord API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. ### Behavior OpenClaw can represent Discord DMs and channel threads through `channel-broker` using stable broker semantics: provider platform `discord`, direct/channel conversation type, conversation id, and optional thread id. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-discord-routing` - Head: `370402e44f` (current Discord proof head) ### Steps 1. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:user:123456789012345678`. 2. Verified the provider request used `platform: "discord"`, `conversation.type: "direct"`, and `conversation.id: "123456789012345678"`. 3. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:channel:222222222222222222?threadId=333333333333333333`. 4. Verified the provider request used channel conversation id plus `threadId`. 5. Resolved the outbound session route for the Discord DM target. 6. Verified the route stayed direct and canonicalized to `discord:direct%3A123456789012345678`. ### Evidence - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 2 files passed, 13 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result Discord DMs and threads now flow through broker target parsing and provider request semantics, not through core or native Discord extension branches. ### Not Tested - Live Discord API traffic; this PR is the opt-in broker migration proof, not a live provider connector. - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2A Channel Broker conformance baseline on top of #86096. - Extends `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` with inbound normalization, dedupe-key, provider capability normalization, and platform capability checks. - Adds bundled broker conformance coverage for durable inbound receive, durable final send receipts, retryable receipt failures, preview/progress capability gates, and `/verbose`/tool-message `channelData`. ## Rollout Context Closes #86109 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86133, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. This PR intentionally does not migrate Telegram, Discord, or Slack routing. Native defaults stay unchanged until the Phase 2B/2C/2D proofs land behind opt-in broker routing. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** The broker conformance baseline normalizes inbound events, builds durable dedupe keys, evaluates preview/progress capabilities, and sends outbound requests through the bundled broker lifecycle without platform-specific core branches. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that imported `src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts`, created a Telegram inbound event, built the durable dedupe key, evaluated Discord preview/progress capabilities, and sent broker outbound requests through `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "inboundDedupeKey": "acme:bot-main:telegram:live-proof-telegram-1", "previewProgressSupported": true } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path produced the expected durable inbound key and capability decision outside the Vitest harness, proving the Phase 2A contract surface can carry the normalized lifecycle facts that later channel migrations consume. - **What was not tested:** Live provider network traffic and the full local suite; those remain covered by later provider proofs and GitHub Actions. ### Behavior The broker contract now has a conformance harness for the lifecycle paths that regress most often across maintained chat channels: inbound normalization/dedupe, durable final sends, provider receipts, retry/failure propagation, preview/progress capability checks, and `/verbose` tool-message metadata. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-conformance` - Head: `f6bf429242` (current Phase 2A head) ### Steps 1. Normalized a synthetic Telegram inbound event through the SDK and accepted it through the durable inbound receive journal. 2. Replayed the same dedupe key while pending and after completion to prove duplicate/redelivery handling. 3. Sent a synthetic Slack final message through the bundled broker runtime and verified provider receipt commit material. 4. Returned a retryable Discord provider receipt and verified the send fails with structured receipt context. 5. Built preview/finalize broker requests and asserted Discord preview/progress support through declared capabilities. 6. Passed `/verbose` tool-message policy through broker `channelData` without platform-specific branching. ### Evidence - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - 1 file passed, 7 tests passed. - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 3 files passed, 10 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result The SDK helpers normalize provider payloads without dropping native metadata, the bundled plugin refuses non-sent provider receipts as durable-send failures, and conformance tests prove the current broker path can represent the Phase 2 lifecycle requirements without changing user-visible channel defaults. ### Not Tested - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. - Live Telegram, Discord, or Slack provider traffic. Those are intentionally scoped to Phase 2B/2C/2D. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 1 protocol foundation
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the narrow foundation. It creates the versioned broker SDK subpath and bundled channel-broker plugin, but it does not move Telegram, Discord, Slack, or any user defaults. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 1 - SDK and bundled broker foundation - Role: Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. - Head: `b95fe03a87` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `b95fe03a87` Focused local validation after the latest Phase 1 package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary Adds Phase 1 of the Channel Broker consolidation program: a versioned `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol surface, a bundled `channel-broker` channel plugin, provider-owned outbound request/receipt helpers, docs, and focused tests. This PR intentionally stays at the protocol/foundation layer. Telegram, Discord, Slack, and later channel migrations are tracked separately and should not be folded into this PR. ## Linked Context Closes #86107 Related #86105 Related #86108 Related #86109 Related #86110 Related #86111 Related #86112 Related #86113 Related #86114 Related #86115 ## Review Repair History The current Phase 1 branch includes focused repairs for ClawSweeper's blocker classes: - Preserves native prefix ownership when `channel-broker` declares platform aliases that overlap native plugins. If Telegram/Discord/Slack are registered, their prefixes stay native-owned; broker aliases own a platform prefix only when no native plugin declares it. - Adds the standard channel SecretRef contract for `channels.channel-broker.{outboundToken,signingSecret}` plus `accounts.*` and `providers.*` variants, and changes the broker schema/docs to use SecretRef-compatible credential input. - Threads `AbortSignal` through broker outbound requests, including the HTTP `fetch` transport and custom runtime override path. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Verified the Phase 1 broker surface resolves as a bundled channel and produces a normalized V1 outbound request for a Telegram-scoped broker target. This proves the user-visible foundation being shipped in this PR: catalog discovery, channel id normalization, target construction, protocol versioning, platform normalization, thread preservation, and final-send payload shape. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, after the current Phase 1 repair/doc series through `d39d47b3db`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); const target = buildBrokerConversationTarget({ platform: "Telegram", conversationId: "chat 123", threadId: "topic-7" }); const request = createBrokerOutboundRequest({ requestId: "send-proof-1", providerId: "synthetic-provider", platform: "Telegram", accountId: "default", conversation: { id: "chat 123", type: "channel", threadId: "topic-7" }, mode: "final", payloads: [{ text: "broker proof" }], requirements: { text: true, thread: true } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));' ``` - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above: ```json { "discoveredChannel": { "id": "channel-broker", "order": 60, "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker" }, "channelNormalization": "channel-broker", "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7", "outboundProtocolVersion": 1, "outboundMode": "final", "outboundPlatform": "telegram", "outboundConversation": { "id": "chat 123", "type": "channel", "threadId": "topic-7" }, "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof" } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** OpenClaw discovered the bundled `channel-broker` entry, normalized the channel id, built a Telegram broker target that preserved `threadId`, and emitted a V1 final outbound request with normalized `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.threadId: "topic-7"`, and payload text `broker proof`. - **What was not tested:** No real third-party broker provider delivery was tested in Phase 1. That is intentionally deferred to the Phase 2 migration proof issues for Telegram, Discord, and Slack. ## Focused Local Validation All local validation below ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: ```bash git diff --check ``` ```bash node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/runtime.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ## Known Inherited Gates - `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check` has an inherited clean-upstream SDK budget drift; this PR adds the intended `plugin-sdk/channel-broker` public subpath. - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` has inherited unrelated glossary-label failures on clean upstream; Channel Broker glossary entries were added in this branch.

target.title: [codex] Add Xiaomi Token Plan provider support
target.body:
## Summary

- add first-class `xiaomi-token-plan` support to the bundled Xiaomi provider plugin
- add regional Token Plan onboarding/auth choices, static v2.5 catalog entries, and `tp-...` / `sk-...` key-shape validation
- wire the new provider through usage/auth/config overlay surfaces and update Xiaomi provider docs

## Why

OpenClaw's bundled Xiaomi plugin only supported pay-as-you-go `xiaomi` setup, while issue #86169 asked for Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan support without requiring manual custom-provider wiring.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xiaomi/index.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/onboard.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice-options.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/auth-choice.contract.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/provider-usage.types.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.ts src/config/zod-schema.core.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/index.ts extensions/xiaomi/index.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/onboard.ts extensions/xiaomi/onboard.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/provider-catalog.ts extensions/xiaomi/openclaw.plugin.json extensions/xiaomi/stream.ts extensions/xiaomi/thinking.ts src/commands/auth-choice-options.test.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw can onboard and resolve Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan as a first-class provider with regional preset endpoints instead of requiring manual custom-provider wiring.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout with focused Xiaomi provider, onboarding, auth-choice, provider-usage, and config overlay contract coverage.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xiaomi/index.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/onboard.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice-options.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/auth-choice.contract.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: `xiaomi-token-plan` now registers regional auth choices, writes the regional base URL during onboarding, exposes a static Token Plan catalog, rejects mismatched key prefixes, and shows up in provider usage/config overlay surfaces.

Observed result after fix: `8` test files passed, `166` tests passed; focused code formatting, docs formatting, and `git diff --check` all passed.

What was not tested: Live Xiaomi Token Plan credentials or real endpoint calls, GitHub Actions / Crabbox / Testbox runs, and Anthropic-compatible Token Plan routes.

Fixes #86169.

target.title: fix(agents): strip plaintext model provider keys
target.body:
## Summary
- strip plaintext provider `apiKey` values after merge-mode preservation, source-managed enforcement, and provider compatibility transforms so old `models.json` keys cannot be reintroduced into the final prompt-facing catalog
- clean legacy catalog-only `models.json` plaintext provider keys before the empty-provider `skip` path can preserve them
- migrate existing `models.json`-only plaintext provider keys into the target agent's local `auth-profiles.json` before writing the stripped catalog when there is no configured provider key and no target-local auth profile for that provider
- keep inherited main-agent profiles and runtime/external overlays from suppressing local legacy `models.json` key migration for non-main agents
- migrate known/env-present legacy env-name catalog credentials such as `LITELLM_KEY` to env-backed auth-profile `keyRef`s, while preserving ambiguous env-shaped plaintext strings as inline local auth-profile keys
- preserve non-secret markers such as env SecretRef markers and custom-provider `secretref-managed` compatibility markers
- preserve current-main plugin metadata discovery and manifest-aware model ID normalization while routing providers through the prompt-safe stripping path
- add regression coverage for generated plaintext stripping, merge-mode stripping, catalog-only cleanup, existing-key migration, fail-closed behavior, inherited-profile migration boundaries, env-present keyRef migration, ambiguous env-shaped key preservation, pi model discovery compatibility, metadata discovery threading, and current-head write-path stabilization

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated and legacy `models.json` files should not persist plaintext provider `apiKey` values. Cleanup should preserve upgrade auth outside the prompt-facing catalog, including non-main agent local migration boundaries and env-shaped legacy values that must only become `keyRef`s when an env marker or observed env value proves they are env-backed. The rebased branch should also preserve current-main plugin metadata discovery and manifest-aware model normalization.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source tree matching PR head `d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702` (`treeMatchesRemoteHead: true`; local commit `7a143b411b76acb8046c5fa1c65823e27d0768e1` has the same tree because the remote commit was created through the GitHub Git Database API), macOS, Node.js `v24.1.0`, `/usr/bin/python3` for fs-safe pinned writes. Provider keys in proof output are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=require FS_SAFE_PYTHON=/usr/bin/python3 REMOTE_HEAD=d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702 REMOTE_TREE=4497822feac55fee0e0442f93328e5da0e423cc5 node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

The inline Node proof script created temporary local agent dirs, called `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`, `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps`, `discoverModels`, `ensureAuthProfileStore`, and `createProviderAuthResolver`, and exercised these real paths: inline custom provider stripping, legacy configured `models.json` key migration, catalog-only cleanup, env-present `keyRef` migration, ambiguous env-shaped plaintext preservation, non-main inherited-profile migration boundary, pi model discovery compatibility, and manifest-aware model normalization after stripping.

- Evidence after fix (copied live output, redacted):

```json
{
  "remoteHead": "d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702",
  "localCommit": "7a143b411b76acb8046c5fa1c65823e27d0768e1",
  "treeMatchesRemoteHead": true,
  "localTree": "4497822feac55fee0e0442f93328e5da0e423cc5",
```

target.title: fix(control-ui): add guarded dashboard shortcuts
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a centralized guarded Control UI dashboard shortcut dispatcher for `/`, `N`, `Esc`, and the existing Cmd/Ctrl+K palette shortcut.
- Reuses existing app actions: `/` switches to Chat and focuses the composer, `N` calls the same New messages scroll path, and `Esc` dismisses transient dashboard UI in a defined priority order.
- Keeps dashboard shortcuts out of inputs, textareas, selects, contenteditable regions, role=textbox controls, and common embedded editor roots.
- Adds visible `N` discoverability on the New messages affordance and focused regression coverage for dispatch, editable no-ops, and the click path.

Fixes #81946.
Refs closed prior implementation attempt #81958.

## Duplicate / related work review
- Current `origin/main` still only has the Cmd/Ctrl+K palette handler and click-only New messages button, so #81946 is not resolved on main.
- GitHub timeline and search found #81958 as the concrete tied PR. It implemented the same issue, passed ClawSweeper/CI, then was closed stale unmerged on 2026-05-16.
- `gitcrawl` was stale for #81946 and had no embedding for the new issue, so live `gh` was used for current issue/PR truth.
- `prtags` is installed and authenticated as BunsDev, but `prtags search text` and `prtags group list` both timed out against `https://prtags.dutiful.dev`, so duplicate tagging could not be written safely in this pass.

## Bug / security review
- UI-only keyboard handling and tests; no dependency, workflow, server, auth, secret, network, filesystem, or permission changes.
- The shortcut guard is centralized in `ui/src/ui/app-keyboard-shortcuts.ts` so dashboard shortcuts do not get scattered across components.
- Security-sensitive review point was key interception: global shortcuts now bail out for editable targets, including contenteditable and embedded editor roots, before dispatching printable dashboard shortcuts.
- `N` only fires on the Chat tab when the New messages affordance is active and no manual refresh is in flight, avoiding page-level/native scroll hijacking.
- `Esc` dismissal is explicit and bounded to existing transient UI state; it does not execute user-controlled strings or invoke navigation outside existing app methods.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Control UI dashboard shortcuts now support `/` composer focus, `N` new-message jump, and `Esc` transient dismissal through a guarded dispatcher while preserving editable-field and composer-local behavior.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on Node/pnpm plus Blacksmith Testbox Linux validation for the rebased branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css ui/src/ui/app-keyboard-shortcuts.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix:
- Focused UI tests passed: 2 files, 57 tests.
- Formatting check passed on all touched files.
- UI test typecheck passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- Testbox changed-file gate passed on `tbx_01ks4gfpat2r553qp4fxpxwbhx`, exit 0, command time 1m57s.

Observed result after fix: Regression tests cover `/` switching to Chat and focusing the composer, `N` calling `scrollToBottom()` only when the new-message state is active, editable targets no-oping for dashboard shortcuts, `Esc` dismissing transient state, and the New messages button retaining its click path while exposing the `N` shortcut.

What was not tested: No manual browser smoke was run after this patch; coverage is automated Control UI browser/unit tests plus Testbox changed gates.

target.title: [Fix] Warm provider auth off main thread
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: gateway provider-auth prewarm could monopolize the main Node event loop during startup, delaying channel handshakes and inbound message handling.
- Solution: move the provider-auth warm path into a worker thread while preserving the prepared auth-state cache contract and cancellation semantics.
- What changed: added a provider-auth worker entry, serialized/published warm snapshots, carried runtime auth-store presence into the worker, and updated startup/reload/logout rewarm call sites.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): model-provider auth semantics, credential persistence, channel startup ordering, and public config defaults are unchanged.

## Motivation

- Closes #85999. The reported 2026.5.22 regression shows provider-auth prewarm taking 58-68s with 36s event-loop stalls on a 2vCPU Linux gateway, causing Discord/Feishu/Telegram startup timeouts and delayed inbound messages.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85999
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: provider-auth warm now executes through a worker-thread runtime path instead of doing the expensive provider/auth sweep on the gateway main event loop.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout and built `dist` output on macOS with Node v24.14.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```sh
  node --import tsx -e "import('./src/agents/model-provider-auth.ts').then(async ({warmCurrentProviderAuthStateOffMainThread, clearCurrentProviderAuthState}) => { await warmCurrentProviderAuthStateOffMainThread({models:{providers:{demo:{baseUrl:'https://example.com/v1', api:'openai', models:[{id:'m'}]}}}}); clearCurrentProviderAuthState(); console.log('ok-default'); })"
  node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const f=fs.readdirSync('dist').find(f=>/^model-provider-auth-.*\.js$/.test(f)); import('./dist/'+f).then(async m=>{await m.a({models:{providers:{demo:{baseUrl:'https://example.com/v1',api:'openai',models:[{id:'m'}]}}}}, {timeoutMs:30000}); m.n(); console.log('ok', f);})"
  ```
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  ok-default
  node --import tsx -e ... 47.43s user 3.84s system 102% cpu 50.221 total

  ok model-provider-auth-OLG_yXSX.js
  ```
- Observed result after fix: both the source-checkout worker path and built dist worker chunk completed successfully and published/cleared provider-auth state without touching channel code.
- What was not tested: the original Azure B2als_v2 host and live Discord/Telegram/Feishu restart path were not available in this local session.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue logs show `provider auth state pre-warmed in 58655ms eventLoopMax=36540.8ms` and `provider auth state pre-warmed in 67840ms eventLoopMax=36876.3ms` on 2026.5.22.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `warmCurrentProviderAuthState` ran the catalog/provider/agent auth discovery sweep on the gateway process main thread after gateway ready. On constrained hosts, external auth discovery and store hydration could stall the event loop long enough to break channel startup timers.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing coverage asserted auth correctness but did not lock the gateway startup/reload/logout call sites to a non-main-thread warm path or require the worker entry in dist/release checks.
- Contributing context (if known): configured agents with multiple providers amplify the per-agent provider-auth sweep.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts`, `src/agents/model-provider-auth.worker.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts`, `src/infra/tsdown-config.test.ts`, `test/release-check.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: worker snapshot publication, stale/cancelled warm suppression, runtime auth-store presence transfer, gateway startup/reload/logout using the off-main-thread warmer, and release/build inclusion of the worker entry.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the ownership boundary where the regression entered without requiring live provider credentials or channel transports.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing auth-state tests covered correctness but not the worker boundary.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A - new tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Gateway startup, reload, and logout-triggered provider-auth rewarm should no longer block channel timers on the main event loop. Auth answers and config semantics remain unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
gateway ready -> main-thread provider auth sweep -> channel timers delayed

After:
gateway ready -> worker-thread provider auth sweep -> snapshot publish -> channel timers keep running
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: runtime auth-store snapshots passed to the worker are reduced to provider-presence-only placeholder credentials, so the worker can preserve auth availability semantics without copying real token material.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node v24.14.0, pnpm workspace scripts, built `dist`
- Model/provider: minimal demo OpenAI-compatible provider config
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A - worker/auth runtime path only
- Relevant config (redacted): one demo provider with one model and no secrets

### Steps

1. Run the focused Vitest set for the touched auth/gateway/release surfaces.
2. Build tsdown dist with the worker entry included.
3. Execute the source-checkout worker warm smoke.
4. Execute the built-dist worker warm smoke.
5. Run pre-ship profile scans.

### Expected

- Provider-auth warm completes through the worker path, the built worker chunk is resolvable, and focused tests pass.

### Actual

- Focused tests passed: 11 files, 214 tests.
- `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs --no-clean` completed successfully.
- Source worker smoke printed `ok-default`.
- Dist worker smoke printed `ok model-provider-auth-OLG_yXSX.js`.
- Pre-ship codex-style, coderabbit-style, and greptileai-style scans returned `NO_FINDINGS`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused auth/gateway/release tests, tsdown build, source worker smoke, built dist worker smoke, pre-ship profile scan.
- Edge cases checked: stale warm cancellation, worker timeout/error handling, invalid worker payloads, runtime auth-store presence transfer, gateway startup/reload/logout call-site rewiring, release-check worker inclusion.
- What you did **not** verify: live affected Linux VM restart and live channel handshakes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A - no migration needed.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: worker startup failures could leave the prepared auth cache cold.
  - Mitigation: failures are logged by existing call sites and `hasAuthForModelProvider` keeps the existing compute fallback when no prepared snapshot matches.
- Risk: runtime-only auth snapshots could leak credential material into worker data.
  - Mitigation: the warm path sends provider-presence-only placeholder credentials, not real token values.
